title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is less about new policy and more about the machinery of AI assurance: new benchmarks, audits, and formal control methods aimed at making model behavior more measurable and governable. The strongest updates focus on evaluation quality, agent security, privacy leakage, and how safety alignment can fail under realistic prompting and deployment conditions.

items.title: OpenAI introduces LifeSciBench for real-world life science model evaluation
items.why_it_matters: Adds an expert-built benchmark for high-stakes scientific capability and risk assessment.

items.title: Google DeepMind outlines an AI Control Roadmap for securing agents
items.why_it_matters: Shows how labs are combining monitoring and access controls for agentic system security.

items.title: OpenAI details physician-informed evaluations for ChatGPT health responses
items.why_it_matters: Health-domain evaluations directly affect deployment standards for safety-critical consumer use.

items.title: Study finds mixed compliance demonstrations can weaken safety-aligned LLM behavior
items.why_it_matters: Clarifies how in-context examples may erode refusal behavior and enable jailbreaks.

items.title: Paper proposes sound probabilistic verification for AI agent policies
items.why_it_matters: Advances formal methods for monitoring uncertain agent behavior under probabilistic policies.

items.title: Audit shows training-free AI image detectors are fragile to preprocessing and compression
items.why_it_matters: Questions the reliability of widely touted detector approaches in realistic deployment settings.

items.title: CWE-Trace study probes whether LLM vulnerability detection reflects reasoning or contamination
items.why_it_matters: Improves security evaluation by separating benchmark memorization from genuine vulnerability understanding.

items.title: Researchers model evaluator bias propagation in multi-agent LLM systems
items.why_it_matters: Highlights a failure mode where biased model judgments cascade through agent networks.

items.title: StylisticBias identifies a small set of visual cues behind social bias in multimodal models
items.why_it_matters: Offers a more targeted basis for auditing and mitigating bias in MLLMs.

items.title: Paper proposes certificate-bound authority for agentic control planes
items.why_it_matters: Separates model reasoning from execution authority to reduce dangerous autonomous actions.

items.title: Federated language model fine-tuning paper reports a privacy backdoor in PEFT setups
items.why_it_matters: Suggests practical privacy leakage risks in supposedly data-protective collaborative training.

items.title: A-COMPASS presents formal foundations for anonymity analysis in microdata
items.why_it_matters: Strengthens privacy assurance by bringing formal verification to anonymization analysis.

items.title: Implicit-feedback alignment paper uses mouse and eye signals as preference data
items.why_it_matters: Raises both scalable alignment possibilities and new privacy concerns in feedback collection.

items.title: Transparency study examines whether DiffusionGemma makes reasoning harder to inspect
items.why_it_matters: Targets interpretability questions for architectures that shift computation into latent spaces.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
